nativeRange.habitat: Shallow subtidal areas with sand, silt or rubble bottoms and seagrass beds; generally reported between roughly 1 and 78 m depth

diet.notes: Generally accepts live foods most readily — feeder shrimp, grass/glass shrimp, and small live fish are commonly used to initiate feeding. Some individuals have been reported to transition onto frozen meaty foods (mysis, krill, silversides) through patient stick-feeding, particularly when conditioned young. Feeding two to four times per week tends to be sufficient given the species' slow, ambush-predator metabolism. Fatty feeder fish such as goldfish are commonly considered unsuitable for long-term use.

summary: A cryptic, leaf-mimicking ambush predator from Indo-Pacific seagrass and rubble flats. Venomous dorsal, anal and pelvic spines require careful handling. Generally kept singly in a 150 L species-focused or peaceful community tank.
careNotes:
The Sailfin/Rogue Scorpion is the aquarium-trade name for the Cockatoo Waspfish, currently accepted as Ablabys taenianotus by FishBase (the older synonym Amblyapistus taenionotus is still widely used by retailers including LiveAquaria). It is a cryptic, slow-moving ambush predator that mimics a drifting dead leaf or piece of seagrass, often rocking back and forth in the bottom surge. Reported color phases include red, yellow, brown, and blue.

Venom: dorsal, anal, and pelvic spines carry venom and have been reported to cause intense pain on contact; handling with bare hands is strongly discouraged, and hot-water immersion is the commonly cited first aid for stings.

Generally considered nocturnal — most active at dusk and after lights-out — which affects feeding schedule (target-feed in the evening) and tankmate choice (avoid fast diurnal competitors that may steal food).

Cross-source disagreements: adult size — LiveAquaria lists 4 in (~10 cm), while FishBase, Quality Marine and Wikipedia report up to 13–15 cm; range used here (10–15 cm). Temperature — LiveAquaria 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); FishBase 23.5–29°C; Wikipedia 23–26°C; averaged to 23–27°C. Minimum tank size — LiveAquaria 30 gal (~114 L), Singapore Reef Club 20 gal (~76 L), Quality Marine 50 gal (~190 L); biased toward the cautious side and rounded to 150 L to accommodate the species' need for ample bottom footprint, hiding places, and slow ambush behavior. Reef safety — LiveAquaria 'with caution', Quality Marine 'yes'; 'with caution' used here because any ornamental shrimp or fish small enough to fit in the mouth is at risk.

Husbandry style: prefers slower flow, soft sand or rubble substrate, and dense rockwork or seagrass to hide and perch among. Water quality should be kept high — these are reported as sensitive to declining conditions. Only one specimen should be kept per tank unless a confirmed mated pair is acquired together.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page for Amblyapistus taenionotus. Provided: size (4 in), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), specific gravity (1.020–1.025), dKH (8–12), minimum tank (30 gal), diet, temperament (semi-aggressive), care level (moderate), reef safe (with caution), color variants (red/yellow/brown/blue), venom note.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked accepted scientific name (Ablabys taenianotus), family (Tetrarogidae), max length 15 cm TL, depth range 1–78 m, temperature range 23.5–29°C, Indo-West-Pacific distribution (Andaman Sea to Fiji, Japan to Australia), nocturnal behavior, solitary/pair grouping.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (~6 in / 15 cm), minimum tank (50 gal), water parameters (consistent with LiveAquaria), diet preference for live shrimp/fish and stick-feeding training, shy temperament. Listed reef safe as 'yes'; majority 'with caution' used instead.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Amblyapistus taenionotus)
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (accepted name Ablabys taenianotus), max size 13–15 cm, distribution (West Pacific from Malaysia to Japan and Indonesia to Australia), benthic nocturnal leaf-mimicking behavior, minimum 30 gal aquarium, temperature 23–26°C, venomous-handling caution.

additional.name: Singapore Reef Club
additional.notes: Cross-checked semi-aggressive temperament, carnivore live-food preference, water parameters (72–78°F, 1.020–1.025 SG, pH 8.1–8.4); listed minimum tank 20 gal which is on the low end of the spread.
